title: Cyclop Tutorial Nr. 1 "The Wobble Knob"
description:
Here is the first of a series of Cyclop Tutorials
made by bigbrainaudio.com guru Brian Smith!

Get to control the power of Cyclop now!

The Cyclop Synth can be purchased from
www.Sugar-Bytes.de
transcript: [Music] welcome to cyclop I'm Brian Smith today the wobble knob the wobble knob can control just about anything but typical use is a wobble base let's check that out I'll start with a a nit bank here and go for the saw regiment which will give me some nice saw tooth waves here and I'll put the velocity on so it's always loud turn off the Glide turn down a little bit the uh Decay time and this sound button doesn't need to be on turn off this filter so I just have one filter on and Route my sound from the synths here all the way to the filter I'll turn up the the saw amount here so we have some nice thick sound and this is basically the parameter I want to control with a kind of a wobble sound so far nothing's really happening but what I want to do is to get this moving here in this window there are a bunch of different Windows here and we'll talk about them maybe someday later but this is the assignment window and what I want to assign is the wobble amount and these are my targets and here's the filter and the first knob these are the three knobs here and the first knob is right here at the cut off and you can see it's blinking at me really nicely at the rate of the wobble knob here so it blinks slowly when I'm at a half note here and faster when I'm in Faster rates so it's really cool and what happens well I'm going to turn down the cut off so that the wobble has something to do cool it works well I could assign that to a controller by right clicking midy learn it's kind of cool and play that and that's not really where the fun is the fun is when you record that uh that movement you know when you're going around so what I'm going to do is just to hit this record button and I start playing and tweaking and now I want to play it back and so as I play it's going to play the same wobble race I can redraw this stuff too or I can shorten my wobble length here or my sequence length or I can set the length right here maybe set up the resonance a little bit so so you got a nice little sound going on there not thicken that up maybe put some kind of effects going on here maybe some chorus or phas or something you know something cool yeah and that's how it works so go try that out and in the meantime I'm just going to chill a little bit [Laughter]
